used_quotes: A good briefing tells you what happened. A great one tells you what people are arguing about.
Ah, but a man's reach should exceed his grasp, / Or what's a heaven for?
All things are an exchange for fire, and fire for all things, as goods for gold and gold for goods.
Better is the end of a thing than the beginning thereof: and the patient in spirit is better than the proud in spirit.
Every day is a journey, and the journey itself is home.
How did you go bankrupt?… Gradually and then suddenly.
How we spend our days is, of course, how we spend our lives.
I turn the wheel that spins; I delight to see the high come down and the low ascend.
In the fields of observation, chance favours only the prepared mind.
Insist on yourself; never imitate.
It is the mark of an educated mind to be able to entertain a thought without accepting it.
Memory is the treasury and guardian of all things.
Nothing in life is to be feared, it is only to be understood.
Peace cannot be kept by force; it can only be achieved by understanding.
Peace hath her victories, no less renown'd than war.
Somewhere, something incredible is waiting to be known.
The best way out is always through.
The condition upon which God hath given liberty to man is eternal vigilance.
The cost of a thing is the amount of what I will call life which is required to be exchanged for it, immediately or in the long run.
The first principle is that you must not fool yourself — and you are the easiest person to fool.
The future is already here — it's just not very evenly distributed.
The map is not the territory.
The old order changeth, yielding place to new, / And God fulfils himself in many ways, / Lest one good custom should corrupt the world.
The owl of Minerva spreads its wings only with the falling of the dusk.
The real voyage of discovery consists not in seeking new landscapes, but in having new eyes.
There are more things likely to frighten us than there are to crush us; we suffer more often in imagination than in reality.
There is a tide in the affairs of men, which, taken at the flood, leads on to fortune; omitted, all the voyage of their life is bound in shallows and in miseries.
Thirty spokes share the wheel's hub; it is the centre hole that makes it useful. … Therefore profit comes from what is there; usefulness from what is not there.
To travel hopefully is a better thing than to arrive, and the true success is to labour.
Two things fill the mind with ever new and increasing admiration and awe, the more often and steadily we reflect upon them: the starry heavens above me and the moral law within me.
War is merely the continuation of policy by other means." — Carl von Clausewitz, *On War* (1832)
We shape our buildings; thereafter they shape us.
Well done is better than well said.
When we try to pick out anything by itself, we find it hitched to everything else in the Universe.
When you can measure what you are speaking about, and express it in numbers, you know something about it … but when you cannot measure it … your knowledge is of a meagre and unsatisfactory kind.
Where is the wisdom we have lost in knowledge? Where is the knowledge we have lost in information?
Whereof one cannot speak, thereof one must be silent.
“Though the mills of God grind slowly, yet they grind exceeding small; / Though with patience He stands waiting, with exactness grinds He all.”

issues.quote: The map is not the territory.
issues.author: Alfred Korzybski
issues.note: Weekend-into-week briefing, 24pp; led on the still-unsigned Hormuz 60-day MOU read five ways (Washington / Tehran / Beijing / Gulf / non-aligned) \& Brent near US$99 (\~17% below a month earlier), with Ukraine's drone-parity turn as the secondary; H200 cleared for China yet booking \~no revenue as Beijing quietly steers firms to Huawei Ascend, DeepSeek V4-Pro's permanent price cut sets a new floor \& the open-weight top-ten goes all-China, SubQ's sub-quadratic LLM; smart-glasses wave (Meta Ray-Ban, Project Aura, Apple's N50 slip), Home Assistant 2026.6 beta \& Ubiquiti's 10-Gig gateways; Watches \& Wonders 2026 (Rolex OP / "100 Years" Rolesor) vs. the Gulf luxury slump; Mandalorian \& Grogu's soft SW opening, "Taiwan Travelogue" Booker; Family on the age-verification era (Australia's under-16 ban as template); Curiosities on 3I/ATLAS hiding in Rubin's pre-discovery frames for ten days; Love \& Life on quality-over-status relationships; Fitness on VO₂ max as a "vital sign" \& exercise snacks; Diary led by the summer matsuri \& teamLab Planets' 2027 close. THE LONG READ: "The Unsigned World" — the gap between map and territory (Korzybski \& Borges's one-to-one map) across the unsigned treaty, the unshipped chip \& the unread comet, via Napoleon's Continental System (1806–14), the 1980s Tanker War / Operation Earnest Will \& Galileo recording Neptune as a "fixed star" (1612–13); callbacks to Feynman (No. 08) \& Proust (No. 07).

issues.quote: The cost of a thing is the amount of what I will call life which is required to be exchanged for it, immediately or in the long run.
issues.author: Henry David Thoreau, *Walden* (1854)
issues.note: Weekend-into-week briefing, 24pp; led on Anthropic's $965B confidential S-1 (1 Jun, narrowly above OpenAI's $852B March mark) \& the Hormuz 60-day MOU returned to Tehran for Trump's third edit (Brent $94.58, −17.4% m/m); Pritam Singh's requisitioned 28 Jun cadre conference with a secret-ballot trigger; the launch of "Screen Smart from the Start" with bescreensmart.gov.sg (only 37% of SG parents confident); HDB Q1 2026 RPI's first dip since 2019 (−0.1% q/q) yet a record 412 $1M flats; Microsoft Build 2026 cuts the OpenAI cord — Project Polaris replaces GPT-4 in Copilot by August, Claude Opus 4.8 \& Sonnet 4.6 land first-party in Azure AI Foundry; MiniMax M3 the first open-weight model to top SWE-Bench Pro at 59.0%; Sony A7R VI ships 4 Jun \& Sennheiser Momentum 5 ships 30 Jun with a screwdriver-replaceable battery; Home Assistant 2026.6 brings Matter onboarding over BLE proxies; Watches \& Wonders centres on Patek's 50th-anniversary Nautilus quartet (2,000-piece 5810/1G) \& the 958G pocket watch; Nolan's $250M all-IMAX-70mm *Odyssey* + Spielberg's *Disclosure Day*; Taylor's Version finale (6 Jun); *Taiwan Travelogue* wins Mandarin's first Booker International; Family Desk on the screen-smart toolkit + APA's gaming risk note; Whisper Desk on Hollywood's separation season (Biggs/Mollen, Daddario/Form confirmed); Curiosities on NASA Goddard's promise of \~100,000 Roman-discovered worlds \& a diamond-core planet; Love \& Life on Tinder's "clear-coding" \& the Wheatley/IFS dating recession; Fitness on the case against over-extending zone 2 (Schoenfeld et al. 2026); Diary led by Sanno Matsuri's even-year procession, Sumida fireworks \& the World Cup opening (11 Jun). THE LONG READ: "What's the Quote?" — Thoreau's cost-of-a-thing anchor pulls three threads (valuation theatre, negotiation as performance, industrialisation of attention) through Newton/South Sea Bubble (1720, Odlyzko on the \~£20,000 loss), Smith's diamond-water paradox (*Wealth of Nations*, 1776), the Vienna Congress \& Panmunjom armistice as "negotiation under continuous costly action," and Lippmann's *Public Opinion* (1922, "The World Outside and the Pictures in Our Heads"); callbacks to Korzybski (No. 09) \& Feynman (No. 08). Editorial theme: *the price of anything*.

issues.quote: The best way out is always through.
issues.author: Robert Frost, *A Servant to Servants* (1914)
issues.note: Weekend Edition, 24pp; led on Iran suspending the Hormuz talks (1 Jun) & Brent snapping back toward $100 (~-15% m/m), with Colombia's 21 Jun runoff (de la Espriella 43.7% vs Cepeda 40.9%) & Ukraine's St Petersburg oil-terminal strike as secondaries; WP's 28 Jun cadres conference & secret-ballot trigger for Pritam Singh, the oil shock reaching SG electricity tariffs (29.72c/kWh, sharper Jul hike flagged); HDB RPI's first dip since 2019 (203.4, -0.1% q/q); Microsoft Build's own-model turn (MAI-Thinking-1, 35B-active/256k) & Beijing waving away the H200 for Huawei Ascend, MiniMax M3 topping open-weight SWE-Bench Pro at 59.0%; Sony A7R VI (66.8MP) ships & Sennheiser Momentum 5 (16 Jun, screwdriver-replaceable battery); Home Assistant 2026.6 local Z-Wave lock PINs + two-way IR; Watches & Wonders' Nautilus-50 / Hermes H08 Skeleton vs the Gulf luxury chill; Toy Story 5's all-Swift musical & the Women's Prize (11 Jun); Family Desk on the age-gate era (KOSA / App Store Act / Australia under-16 ban) + a "don't be alarmist" box; Curiosities on 3I/ATLAS's colder-sun water & its summer farewell (almost named 3I/Rubin); Love & Life on the dating recession & the Harvard relationships finding; Fitness on muscle-as-index-fund & the VO2-max-overstated sceptic; Diary led by the even-year Sanno Matsuri (7-17 Jun) & BTS's four-night Singapore stand (tickets 5 Jun). THE LONG READ: "The Toll and the Way Around" -- chokepoints & the bypass, via the Oresund Sound Dues (1429-1857, bought out by the 1857 Copenhagen Convention), Vasco da Gama's 1498 Cape route around Venice, & Robert Fortune's 1848 theft of tea; callbacks to Thoreau (No. 10) & Korzybski (No. 09). Editorial theme: *through, or around*.

issues.quote: To travel hopefully is a better thing than to arrive, and the true success is to labour.
issues.author: Robert Louis Stevenson, *El Dorado* (1881)
issues.note: Weekend Edition, 24pp; led on the Hormuz peace "described in every capital and signed in none" (Iran's stalled messaging vs. Rubio's "talks still live") & oil's relief-to-fear whiplash (Brent ~US$95, ~-10% m/m), with Colombia's 21 Jun runoff & Ukraine's oil-infra drone war as secondaries; WP's 28 Jun secret-ballot cadres conference over Pritam Singh & the oil shock in SG tariffs (29.72c/kWh, sharper Q3 hike flagged); HDB RPI's first dip since 2019 (203.4, -0.1% q/q) beside a record 400+ million-dollar flats; the H200 cleared for China yet selling ~nothing as Beijing steers to Huawei Ascend, Anthropic's confidential ~$965B S-1 (no date) topping OpenAI's $852B, MiniMax M3 leading open weights at SWE-Bench Pro ~59%; Sony a7R VI ships (4 Jun) & Sennheiser Momentum 5 (mid-Jun) vs. Fujifilm's first cameraless June; Home Assistant 2026.6 "Pick a card" + two-way IR & Z-Wave lock PINs (legacy template syntax removed); Watches & Wonders' Nautilus-50 & Sotheby's 15 Jun "Shapes of Cartier" vs. the boutique chill; Toy Story 5 / Nolan's all-IMAX *Odyssey* & the Women's Prize (11 Jun); Family on the age-verification era (Australia under-16, US state laws, KOSA) + a "don't be alarmist" box; Whisper on Hollywood's separation season (all flagged); Curiosities on 3I/ATLAS leaving forever & SETI's Allen-Array silence ("the looking was the win") + JWST-MIRI methanol; Love & Life on relationship quality beating singlehood & the Harvard study; Fitness on VO2 max as the #1 longevity marker & muscle as "magic sauce" + a peptide-hype sceptic box; Diary led by BTS's four-night National Stadium return (first 7-member show in 7 years) & the summer matsuri. THE LONG READ: "The city that was never there" — the things that refuse to arrive (an unsigned peace, a refused chip, a passing comet, a dateless IPO) and why the hopeful travelling toward them is where the living happens, via Stevenson's *El Dorado* (1881), Ralegh's Manoa/Orinoco hunt (1595/1617), Halley's comet & Le Gentil's two failed transits of Venus (1761/1769); callbacks to Korzybski (No. 09), Thoreau (No. 10) & Frost (No. 11); the one real arrival being BTS's homecoming. Editorial theme: *travel hopefully*.

issues.quote: Well done is better than well said.
issues.author: Benjamin Franklin, *Poor Richard's Almanack* (1737)
issues.note: Weekend Edition, 24pp; led on the Hormuz 60-day MOU "described in every capital and signed in none" (Iran suspended talks 1 Jun over Israel's Lebanon push; Hezbollah reportedly accepted a US ceasefire; Trump: deal reachable "over the next week," needs "a few more points") & oil's whipsaw above US$100 then back to ~US$97 Brent (−12% m/m, 6th straight US inventory draw), with Ukraine's drone on Putin's St Petersburg forum (+ Volgograd refinery offline) & Colombia's 21 Jun runoff (de la Espriella 43.7% vs Cepeda 40.9%) as secondaries; WP's 28 Jun secret-ballot cadres conference over Pritam Singh (account→resign→ballot) & the oil shock reaching the July electricity tariff (29.72c/kWh, sharper hike flagged); the SORA-trough refinancing window (3M SORA ~1%) & June BTO (6,900 flats); the H200 cleared for China yet ~undelivered as Beijing steers to Huawei, the open-weight surge (DeepSeek V4 Pro / Qwen 3.7 Max / Kimi) & frontier-class models now running on desk hardware (DGX Spark, M5 Max, RTX Pro 6000); FUJIKINA & the expected Fujifilm X-T6 (40MP) + Leica's own sensor silicon; Home Assistant local-first (OpenThread BR, native mDNS, Matter sirens); Sotheby's "Shapes of Cartier" 15 Jun & Christie's Patek 3448G "Red Dot"; Olivia Rodrigo's 3rd album (12 Jun) & Taylor Swift's Toy Story 5 single; Family on the age-verification era (Australia under-16, KOSA) + parental-habits-beat-bans + a VPN-spike "don't be alarmist" box; Whisper's engagement/split season (all flagged); Curiosities on JWST's first mid-infrared fingerprint of 3I/ATLAS (the encore) & June's Venus–Jupiter conjunction; Love & Life on relationship quality over status (Folk & Dunn 2026); Fitness on rapamycin possibly blunting exercise gains (Stanfield et al. 2026) & banking VO₂ max early + a sceptic box; Diary led by BTS's National Stadium homecoming & the summer matsuri (even-year Sanno, Gion, Tenjin) + a teamLab art strip. THE LONG READ: "The Said and the Done" — Franklin's maxim on the gap between announcement and execution, via the Kellogg–Briand Pact (1928, war renounced by ~62 signatures with no enforcement), Prohibition / the Volstead Act (1920–33, a ban that manufactured its own evasion → age-gates & VPNs, the cleared-but-unshipped H200) & the Potemkin-village legend (1787, itself likely fabricated — a fake about fakery → vaporware), with the inversion that the week's real deeds (Ukraine's leverage, open weights actually running, the quiet homecomings) outran their words; callbacks to Stevenson (No. 12), Frost (No. 11), Thoreau (No. 10) & Korzybski (No. 09). Editorial theme: *the said and the done*.

issues.quote: In the fields of observation, chance favours only the prepared mind.
issues.author: Louis Pasteur (lecture, University of Lille, 1854)
issues.note: Weekend Edition, 24pp; led on the US–Iran nuclear framework "described in every capital, signed in none" (IAEA's Grossi signals it's close, fresh US oil-smuggling sanctions, Trump's "a few more points"; Iran's negotiator: the winner is whoever is better prepared the day after it's signed) & Brent's nervous retreat to ~US$93–95 (−6% m/m, 6th straight US inventory draw, Chinese imports a 10-yr low, an Oman terminal blast), with Colombia's 21 Jun runoff (de la Espriella 43.7% vs Cepeda 40.9%) & Ukraine's St Petersburg oil-terminal drone strike as secondaries; SG's S$500 CDC vouchers pulled forward to June on the oil shock (Siow: no blunt across-the-board fuel-duty cut), Q1 GDP revised up to 6% on AI demand, April CPI 1.8%; the SORA-trough refinancing window (3M SORA ~1.08%, flagged closing toward ~1.39% by year-end) & HDB RPI −0.1% Q1 (first dip in ~7 yrs); the H200 licensed for China yet ~undelivered as Beijing steers to Huawei, CFR/Setser noting Huawei's own roadmap trails (no true H200-class part before the Ascend 960, late 2027) & a US AI-power-bill angle, the open-weight reshuffle (GLM-5.1 strongest all-rounder / Kimi K2.6 leads GPQA / DeepSeek V4-Pro long-context) with MiniMax M3 demoted; Oura Ring 5 ships (4 Jun, US$399, blood-pressure "Health Radar") & Kuo's cut-to-two Apple glasses roadmap (Vision Air scrapped) vs Google's audio-first Android XR glasses; Matter 1.5 cameras finally on shelves (CSA "Unify" 16–18 Jun, SmartThings first) & Ubiquiti's UniFi OS 5.1.15 Blueprint engine; Japan "still cheap, quietly cooling" (record 42.7m in 2025, ~−3% in 2026, overtourism management) & the watch-auction barometer (Christie's 10 Jun / Sotheby's "Shapes of Cartier" 15 Jun — results, not previews); the Women's Prize (11 Jun, Choi's *Flashlight*) & a quiet-arrivals / loud-anticipation culture slate (Toy Story 5, Nolan's *Odyssey*, Olivia Rodrigo demoted); Family on the evidence toolkit (Nagata/ABCD; monitoring & modelling over reward/punishment; tween 5h33m/day) + the globalising age-gate (Australia's ~4.7m closed child accounts, live AI-chatbot codes) + a "don't be alarmist" box; Whisper's engagement/split season (all flagged); Curiosities on JWST's dawn rock-clouds on WASP-94A b & the lemon-shaped pulsar planet (the prepared instrument; 3I/ATLAS now closed out); Love & Life on company-beats-solitude (Folk & Dunn 2026) & the Harvard study — social capital as a banked reserve; Fitness on banking fast-twitch power (HL-VIRT, *Experimental Gerontology* 2026) + creatine 3–5g & a longevity-discourse sceptic box; Diary led by the even-year summer matsuri (Sanno 7–17 Jun, Gion, Tenjin, Sumida fireworks) + an SG concert/art slate (BTS demoted to an undated callback). THE LONG READ: "Dig the Well Before the Thirst" — reserves laid down before the moment that demands them, via Joseph's seven-year grain store (Genesis 41), Rome's *cura annonae* & the *horrea* granaries, the US Strategic Petroleum Reserve (EPCA 1975, after the 1973–74 embargo) & Pasteur's prepared mind (the 1879 chicken-cholera serendipity → attenuated vaccine); callbacks to Frost (No. 11), Stevenson (No. 12) & Franklin (No. 13). Editorial theme: *the prepared mind / reserves before the need*.

issues.quote: Ah, but a man's reach should exceed his grasp, / Or what's a heaven for?
issues.author: Robert Browning, *Andrea del Sarto* (1855)
issues.note: **The Weekend Edition** — first Sunday lifestyle-mode issue; continuity-correct vs No. 14. 24pp geared lifestyle: cover & teasers led from Curiosities / Good Life / Kit, hard-news desks run lean. THE WORLD (2pp, TWO viewpoint panels): the Hormuz framework still "described in every capital, signed in none" — stalled over Lebanon, US escort suspended, Polymarket signing odds ~72%→~6%, Brent ~$93 — plus a reflective "slow news of a summer Sunday" digest (Colombia 21 Jun run-off; Ukraine St Petersburg/Volgograd strikes). SINGAPORE: the $500 CDC vouchers pulled forward to June (~S$1bn drawer plan, Siow) read as a household relief calendar; Q1 GDP 6%, Apr CPI 1.8%. PROPERTY: the 3M-SORA trough (~1.08%→~1.39% year-end) as a weekend refinancing job; HDB RPI −0.1% Q1. TECH: WWDC eve (8 Jun; Gemini-Siri, "27" betas, rumoured HomePad, Cook's likely-last keynote → Ternus Sept) + the home lab as a weekend project (open-weight field unchanged → "still on the shelf"; H200 one-line delta). KIT: Sony Xperia 1 VIII (NEW — jack/microSD/shutter, 48MP×3, ~S$1,989) + a "still on the shelf" strip (Oura 5 / a7R VI / Momentum 5 / smart-glasses; screwdriver-battery detail retired). CONNECTED HOME: a lifestyle "house that knows it's Sunday" feature (slow-morning scene, circadian lighting, local-first HA) — Matter 1.5 cameras CORRECTLY demoted to a one-line callback (No. 14), not re-led. GOOD LIFE: travel (Japan on a soft yen) + watch CULTURE (>40% of Sotheby's buyers under 40; cooling top-end as connoisseurship) — the NY watch week demoted to a one-line "read the results, not the previews." SCREEN & SOUND: a weekend "what to stream" (The Bear, House of the Dragon, the Lopez/Goldstein Netflix rom-com) + Rodrigo ADVANCED via a NEW angle (her Robert Smith duet revealed at Primavera Sound) + a one-line Women's Prize calendar note. FAMILY (2pp): a warm "screen-free Sunday" (SG June holidays; sleep/play/curiosity over controls; age-gates demoted to a one-liner) + a "don't be alarmist" evidence page. WHISPER: the Swift–Kelce summer-wedding rumour + engagement/split round-up (all flagged). CURIOSITIES: the Venus–Jupiter conjunction (8–9 Jun, ~1.6°, closest until 2028) as a free weekend assignment + the JWST cosmic-web map (5 Jun). LOVE & LIFE: anticipation/savoring (the pre-event peak). FITNESS: the exercise + rapamycin/lamivudine trial & a creatine RCT (Salem et al.) as longevity-lifestyle + the VO₂max-as-proxy sceptic. DIARY (centrepiece, 2pp): the conjunction as a free marquee + i Light / Children's Season + a NEW "where to eat this weekend" block (Tokyo cult imports Hikiniku to Come @ VivoCity & Mensho X @ Raffles Pl; Bouillon Gavroche; Paul Pairet's Good Brunch) + the Tokyo summer-matsuri window (soft yen) + art; BTS demoted to a "now booking" callback (confirmed 17/19/20/22 Dec, tickets from 3 Jun). PHOTO EDITION heroes (verified from Commons pages): Singapore Marina Bay (CC BY-SA 4.0, blieusong); Strait of Hormuz USCGC (PD, USCG — representative); Moon–Venus–Jupiter conjunction (CC BY 3.0, Pendethan — representative, leads Curiosities); Gion Matsuri (CC BY 2.0, born1945 — representative, leads the Diary). THE LONG READ: "The Eve of Arrival" — anticipation as the productive gap between announcement and arrival, via Tantalus, the Millerite "Great Disappointment" (22 Oct 1844) & Le Verrier/Adams' prediction and Galle's discovery of Neptune within ~1° (23 Sep 1846); callbacks to Stevenson (No. 12) & Frost (No. 11). Editorial theme: *the eve / reach exceeds grasp*.

issues.quote: Memory is the treasury and guardian of all things.
issues.author: Cicero, *De Oratore* (55 BC)
issues.note: First true **Weekday Edition** since the Sunday No. 15 (full twelve-desk weekday book, 24pp). Issue spine: *the memory supercycle — memory as the contested treasury*. THE WORLD (2pp, side-by-side viewpoints): the US–Iran framework still "described in every capital, signed in none" — Hezbollah's rejection of the US Lebanon ceasefire named as the obstacle, Iran warning US bases are "no longer safe," Polymarket's signed-by-15-Jun odds collapsing ~60%→~6%; Brent ~US$93 (−4.5% m/m, backwardated, China imports a 10-yr low, 6th straight inventory draw, an Oman Mina al-Fahal blast); secondaries = Colombia's 21 Jun runoff (de la Espriella 43.7% vs Cepeda 40.9%, Valencia's endorsement) & Ukraine's long-range strikes on Russian refineries (Volgograd offline). SINGAPORE: the WP's 28 Jun secret-ballot cadres conference over Pritam Singh — DELTA = Yee Jenn Jong's public "my vote is not secret, I'll back Pritam" at his book launch (3 Jun) + the Law Society's disciplinary referral to the Court of Three Judges; the relief calendar (S$500 CDC vouchers June, July U-Save/S&CC, Aug GSTV, Sept COL) tied to the energy squeeze (Siow); Q1 GDP 6%, Apr CPI 1.8%. PROPERTY: the 3M-SORA trough (~1.08%, modelled to bottom ~1% then ~1.39% by year-end; floating ~1.26% all-in / fixed ~1.35%, both < HDB 2.6%) as a refinancing window; HDB RPI 203.4, −0.1% Q1 (first dip since 2019) beside record million-$ flats; land betterment +4.1%. TECHNOLOGY (2pp): the LEAD — DRAM contract +58–63% q/q (NAND +70–75%), the sharpest in a decade, HBM eating 3–4× wafer area, Framework +50% DDR5, Micron retiring Crucial, counterfeit DDR5, IDC "permanent reallocation" until 2027–28 capacity; WWDC keynote tonight (Gemini-Siri custom ~1.2T-param model, ~$1B/yr to Google, Cook's likely-last keynote → Ternus 1 Sept, "All systems glow," six "27" betas, iPhone 11 dropped, $250M Siri-delay settlement); the home lab — memory as the binding constraint (DGX Spark 128GB $4,699 vs Strix Halo ~half price within ~52–56 tok/s on 120B; a 4-node Mac Studio cluster ~$30k/250W vs 4×H100), open-weight frontier UNCHANGED → "still on the shelf"; H200 delta only. THE KIT: hi-fi LEAD — Klipsch Heritage "Rebellion" (first bookshelf in the Heritage line, reviving PWK's 1958 H8) & Schiit "Vestri" (first portable USB-C DAC/amp), B&W elite refresh; demoted strip (Xperia 1 VIII, Oura 5, a7R VI, Momentum 5). CONNECTED HOME: the case for local-first ahead of WWDC's rumoured HomePad + Gemini-Siri; HA 2026.6 "Pick a card" (shipped 3 Jun) NOT re-led; CSA "Unify" 16–18 Jun watch. issues.quote: There are more things likely to frighten us than there are to crush us; we suffer more often in imagination than in reality.

issues.note: **Weekday Edition** (full twelve-desk weekday book, 24pp). Issue spine: *the imagined and the real — fear & imagination running ahead of reality*. issues.quote: Peace hath her victories, no less renown'd than war.

issues.note: **Weekday Edition** (full twelve-desk weekday book, 24pp; returns to news-forward balance after the No. 21/22 weekend pair). Issue spine: *the thing that finally arrived — arrival is a beginning, not an end; the victories of peace are the day-after work*. THE WORLD (2pp, four viewpoints Tehran/Washington/Beijing/Vienna-IAEA): the LEAD — after 22 editions "described in every capital, signed in none," a US–Iran peace DEAL IS REACHED (announced Sun 14 Jun by Pakistan PM Sharif; signing ceremony set Fri 19 Jun, Switzerland; Hormuz to reopen "toll-free," blockade lifted, frozen Iranian assets released, a reconstruction clause written in), with the day-after caveat front-loaded (Morgan Stanley: mine-clearing, restarting idled fields & re-pricing insurance take months — "a deal you can't yet ship"); oil eased (~US$86 Brent, still ~+23% vs the ~US$70 pre-war floor); secondaries = World Bank's 2026 global-growth cut to 2.5%, Colombia's 21 Jun runoff (de la Espriella 43.7% v Cepeda 40.9%), Ukraine's Volgograd-refinery drone strike. Issue spine: *the eloquence of the unsaid / strength in restraint — across the book, the decisive move this week is the one held back: the statement not made, the model that tells the cloud nothing, the watch that whispers, the silence that either includes or excludes. issues.note: **Weekday Edition** (full twelve-desk weekday book, 24pp; news-forward; no Travel Desk; The Macro Desk standing). Issue spine: *the verdict comes later* — judgement and understanding arrive only in retrospect; the discipline of "too new to call" / "awaiting the print." THE WORLD (2pp): the LEAD — **the ceremony was cancelled, the road map was signed anyway** — US–Iran negotiators at the Bürgenstock resort (Mon 22 Jun) announced a "road map" + immediate technical talks + a Hormuz communication line; Vance claimed IAEA inspectors readmitted ("first step to permanently ending a weapons programme"), Tehran contradicted within hours ("current procedures" / 2024 law only) — a peace disputed before the ink dried; ~440 kg 60%-HEU + centrifuges + verification all DEFERRED into the 60-day window; Lebanon deconfliction cell (Israel not a party); Brent <$80 (3-mo low, first Saudi cargoes through Hormuz). p4 = four viewpoint panels (Washington/Tehran/London/Beijing) + the SECONDARY **Starmer resigns** (Mon 22 Jun; the 174-seat 2024 landslide curdled in ~18 months; caretaker; nominations 9–16 Jul, successor by 1 Sep; favourite Andy Burnham won the Makerfield by-election ~55%; 7th UK leader in a decade) + a **Passages** box on **Alan Greenspan, 1926–2026** + China sanctions 10 US firms + Colombia (de la Espriella confirmed) + Ukraine one-line. SINGAPORE: the LEAD — **the answer kept for 2027** — the civil-nuclear/SMR go/no-go deferred to 2027 pending an IAEA readiness review (19 areas); US/Japan/Korea frameworks, EMA–KHNP MOU, S$10bn Future Energy Fund; Asia Nuclear Energy & SMR 2026 summit in SG 30 Jun–1 Jul; May CPI delta (Apr 1.8% headline/1.4% core, transport +7%; RHB/UOB see core ~2.5% by Aug); NODX +38.4% one-line; WP/Pritam one-line; LTA-phishing scam note. PROPERTY: the LEAD — **the market is waiting for July to set the price** — developer sales 447 in May (3-mo low); the **Bayshore Drive** mixed-use GLS (tender noon 15 Jul, ~S$1.15–1.25bn / ~S$1,150–1,250 psf ppr; first parcel SingHaiyi–Garnet S$1,388 psf ppr Mar) = the East-Coast benchmark; **Sembawang Drive EC** (~450 units) = first tender-test of the 8 May EC overhaul (No. 28 thread); SORA one-line; HDB RPI Q1 −0.1%. TECHNOLOGY (2pp): the LEAD — **"irrational exuberance," priced in trillions** — Greenspan's 1996 phrase reborn as the AI-capex bubble debate (~US$725bn 2026 capex / ~US$2.5tn total AI spend); the **dependency paradox** (the build-out is now too large to pop without taking GDP with it); Fed FSR lists AI valuations among top systemic risks, MAS flags an "abrupt correction in the AI boom." p8 Home Lab — **the cheaper bench**: Ollama's MLX engine (June, faster/leaner on Apple Silicon), LM Studio MLX v1.8.x KV-cache checkpointing (reclaims the long-agentic memory tax, No. 24 thread), incremental GGUF/runtime refinements; 24GB sweet spot = Gemma 4 31B / Qwen 3.6 at 4-bit; H200/China one-line. THE KIT: **the gear that survives the phone** (Gear Patrol thesis — survivors retreat into premium materials/single-mindedness; "support life is the under-disclosed spec"); value pick **Soundcore Liberty 5 Pro Max** (dedicated audio chip, Adaptive ANC 4.0, 1.78" AMOLED case); quirky **Pebble Index 01** voice-ring ("external memory"); grace note **Citizen Eco-Drive Photon** (50th-anniv light-powered, ties the light/cosmic spine); "still on the shelf" = Sony 1000X ColleXion / Snap Specs / Meze Arta / WiiM Bar. THE GOOD LIFE: the OBJECT (rotated to AUDIO) = **Focal Diva Alta Utopia** (new active-wireless flagship atop the Diva range, Focal drivers + Naim, >US$69k; "own, don't rent") + the soft-yen window (USD/JPY ~¥161.5 near 1986 lows, BoJ ~1%, SGD/JPY ~¥126 derived) + Japan tax deltas (departure levy 1 Jul; tax-free switch 1 Nov). THE FAMILY DESK (2pp): the LEAD — **the "molecules of emotional health" may be the grandparents** (Dr Kenneth Barish, Weill Cornell; *The Art and Science of Parenting and Grandparenting*, Routledge 2026; ScienceDaily/Taylor & Francis 13 Jun; 2025 meta-analysis, 20 studies / 11,434 children: warm grandparenting → depression r≈−0.33, anxiety r≈−0.12; the sharper point = unintentional criticism / achievement pressure, not over-praise, is the bigger risk in affluent homes); deliberately NOT screen-time (saturation avoided); "don't be alarmist" + "try this week" boxes; general guidance, not medical advice. THE MACRO DESK ("The Big Picture", standing): framed via **Greenspan's death** (the maestro who cut to 1% buried as successors hold 3.50–3.75% and price a hike; "Fedspeak"; history vindicated Born, not the Maestro); the chain — FOMC hawkish HOLD 3.50–3.75% (17 Jun, Warsh debut, ~130-word statement, a majority projecting ≥1 hike) → Oct hike ~fully priced → US 10Y ~4.50% / 2Y ~4.20% → DXY bid → Brent <$80, gold ~US$4,190/oz (off ~8% on the month from the Jan ATH, +~24% y/y) → USD/JPY ~¥161.5; regime = **higher-for-longer / dollar-bid / risk-capped** (named No. 28, delta only); May PCE due Thu 25 Jun = "awaiting the print"; standing close = positive equity–bond correlation, diversification doing less for **VWRA & global bonds**; education-not-advice, no fabricated levels. CURIOSITIES: the LEAD — **Einstein's "biggest blunder," reconsidered** (the 1917 cosmological constant disowned, revived as dark energy 1998; a new theory links quantum gravity to an exotic quantum state of matter to explain its smallness — flagged THEORETICAL); secondary — slow-growing **T. rex** (~40 yrs to full size, 17 specimens, 22 Jun) + a "neutrino factory" galaxy (19 Jun) one-line + long-lived-families genetics tie. LOVE & LIFE: the LEAD — **the long arithmetic of love** (the "inevitable decline" of long marriages is more myth than measurement; stability + repair/responsiveness/trust as the real predictors — framed honestly as synthesis, correlational); the human anchor = Greenspan–Andrea Mitchell's 29-year marriage ("the joy of my life"). FITNESS ("The Long Game"): the LEAD — **what you inherit vs what you earn** (long-lived-families rare variants, one tempering inflammation/delaying disease, ScienceDaily 21 Jun — the inherited slice; vs the trainable levers; ACSM position-stand callback No. 28; strength sweet-spot 90–119 min/wk → ~13% all-cause / 19% CVD / 27% neurological mortality, published 12 Jun, as a SATURATED callback datapoint only); general guidance, not medical advice. THE DIARY (2pp): the marquee ★ = **BIGBANG, National Stadium Sat 17 Oct 2026** — GLOBAL on-sale Fri 26 Jun 10am local for US/Europe dates = this-week hook; the SG on-sale STILL pending (register b.stage; honesty box; the "11 Sep" figure is the New Jersey date); SG (Dragon Boat 27–28 Jun, GastroBeats/World Cup screenings, NDP 9 Aug) + Japan summer (Mt Fuji opens 1 Jul, Tanabata 7 Jul, Gion 17 & 24 Jul) + a planning-notes box. THE TABLE (rotated, fresh): LEAD **Asin** (chef Ace Tan's progressive-Asian comeback after Asu, too-new); **Obongzip** 2nd outlet at The Star Vista (K-BBQ queue heads west); **Between Buns Deli** (cult sandwich pop-up → permanent CBD deli, Robinson Rd, oxtail melt ~S$23); "now reviewed" callback **Bouillon Gavroche** (No. 28 "On the Radar," first reviews in); honesty rule; prior venues not re-listed. PHOTO EDITION heroes (4, all verified from their Commons file pages): Marina Bay at dusk — File:Singapore Marina Bay Dusk 2018-02-27.jpg, CC BY-SA 4.0, Benh Lieu Song (Singapore); a server rack — File:Servers in a Rack.jpg, CC BY-SA 3.0, Abigor, honestly "representative" (Technology/Home Lab); **Alan Greenspan official portrait — File:Alan Greenspan color photo portrait.jpg, Public Domain (US Federal Reserve), actual subject** (The Macro Desk); Mount Fuji from Lake Kawaguchi — File:Mount Fuji from Lake Kawaguchi (2015-10-26).jpg, CC BY-SA 4.0, Alpsdake (the Diary/Japan). The owl-of-Minerva/dusk cover hero, the 60-day-clock figure, the protective-circle figure, the Macro market tables and all conceptual/data figures kept as original SVG. THE LONG READ (3pp): **"The Owl of Minerva"** — Hegel's line drawn through the week's late verdicts, anchored to Solon & Croesus (Herodotus, *Histories* I — "call no man happy until the end"), the 1720 Mississippi & South Sea Bubbles (John Law hailed then ruined), Newton's "madness of people" (flagged ATTRIBUTED), and Einstein's cosmological constant (blunder → dark energy); the landing — a dawn-reporting magazine cannot fly with the owl but can refuse to mistake daylight for dusk: mark the known, flag the unknown, leave the verdict to the end; Solon's caution = diversification (VWRA). Callbacks Curran (No. 28), Muir (No. 27), Wittgenstein (No. 26). Editorial theme: *the verdict comes later / judgement in retrospect / humility before an unfinished present*.

issues.note: **Weekday Edition** (full twelve-desk weekday book, 24pp; news-forward; no Travel Desk; The Macro Desk standing). Issue spine: *the opening of the line — the difference between a ribbon-cutting and a thing that actually carries load; ride it / find the gold spike / read the print*. THE WORLD (2pp, four viewpoints Washington/Tehran/Beijing/London): the LEAD — after the cancelled Lucerne ceremony (No. 27), US & Iran signed a **road map** to a final deal within 60 days at the Bürgenstock resort (high-level committee + working groups on nuclear/sanctions/dispute-resolution; a Lebanon de-confliction cell), but the deal's first claim cracked within hours — Vance said Iran agreed to readmit IAEA inspectors & that US/Qatar would approve use of unfrozen assets; Tehran contradicted both ("current procedures" only; "Iran is the only country who decides"); Pezeshkian: "never back down from the right to enrich"; Brent <US$80 (~$77, 3-mo low) as Hormuz reopened (>30M bbl/week, first Saudi cargoes, US 60-day oil licence); Pentagon seeks US$80bn for the war. SECONDARY — **Starmer resigned** (Mon 22 Jun; nominations 9–16 Jul, new PM by 1 Sep if contested; 7th UK leader in a decade) + Lebanon truce frayed (2 killed Tue) + Colombia confirmed (de la Espriella 43.7% v Cepeda 40.9%) + Ukraine refinery strikes one-line. SINGAPORE: the LEAD — **the East Coast joins the network**: Thomson–East Coast Line **Stage 4 (Tanjong Rhu–Bayshore)** opened to passengers 23 Jun (free preview 21 Jun) — the East Coast's first MRT spine, the edition's spine in concrete (a line that bears load day one), clean hand-off to Property/Bayshore; + retirement age 63→64 / re-employment 68→69 from 1 Jul; May CPI delta (Apr 1.8%/1.4% core; core ~2.5% by Aug per RHB/UOB); NODX +38.4% one-line; nuclear/SMR parked at 2027 (Asia Nuclear & SMR summit SG 30 Jun–1 Jul); STI record ~5,217. PROPERTY (pivoted off rates, saturated): the LEAD — **the price lands on 15 July**: with Bayshore newly on the rail map, the **Bayshore Drive mixed-use GLS** (tender noon 15 Jul, ~S$1,150–1,250 psf ppr est.; first parcel SingHaiyi–Garnet S$1,388 Mar) becomes the East-Coast benchmark a paused market awaits (developer sales 447 in May, 3-mo low; URA Q1 +0.9% final; HDB resale −0.1%); construction-cost floor (>S$2,400 psf break-even) as structural support; Sembawang Drive EC (first test of the 8 May overhaul, No. 28) + 2H GLS + SORA "still on the shelf." TECHNOLOGY (2pp): the LEAD (Silicon & Sovereignty) — **frontier-class AI boards at consumer prices**: AMD Ryzen AI "Halo"/Strix Halo dev platform at Micro Center (pre-orders Jun; 128GB unified, 70B-class local inference at ~half the DGX Spark price) as the consumer answer to the memory tax (No. 24), set against the **circular-financing** scrutiny that drove Monday's 22 Jun tech sell-off (Nvidia↔OpenAI ~$100bn loop — "capital that circulates vs capital that carries"); H200/China one-line. p8 (The Home Lab): the build — **Gemma 4 12B** (3 Jun, fits 16GB, encoder-free multimodal) + Ollama 0.30 (Jun GGUF/MLX) + LM Studio MLX KV-cache (No. 29) into Home Assistant; the 24GB sweet spot (Qwen 3.6 4-bit); "16GB is the new floor"; open-weight frontier "still on the shelf." THE KIT: the LEAD — **Audio-Technica ATH-WP900SE × Fujigen** (closed-back wired; earcups in Fujigen's guitar maple + 3-tone sunburst lacquer, first time on a headphone) as the craft object "indifferent to the AI-hardware cycle"; secondary Microsoft Surface Pro (Qualcomm X2, ~53% graphics) + a Fluance turntable; "still on the shelf" (Sony 1000X ColleXion, Soundcore Liberty 5 Pro Max, Snap Specs, Meze Arta, WiiM Bar, Xreal R1). CONNECTED HOME: the LEAD — **"now you can see your own mesh"**: Home Assistant's 23 Jun rebuild of its Matter engine onto **matter.js** (open-source TS), upgrade to **Matter 1.5.1**, a new **Thread mesh visualisation** + OTBR Thread 1.4 — the local-first reliability upgrade ("see the hops you own"); Matter 1.6/Joint Fabric one-line (No. 26, announced ≠ adopted); HA 2026.6 continuity. THE GOOD LIFE: the LEAD — **the soft-yen Japan window** (USD/JPY ~¥161.5, near 1980s lows; SGD/JPY ~¥126; early-July connoisseur window; tax-free reform 1 Nov) + an honest-caveat box (a soft yen is the visitor's gift & the host's strain); the OBJECT (rotated to an independent) = **Atelier Wen Perception V3 "Yún"** (13 Jun; hand-guilloché, the thing made better by iteration) — watches/audio saturation avoided; GS-in-Ginza one-line. SCREEN & SOUND: the LEAD — **Supergirl's opening is the verdict** (Milly Alcock / Jason Momoa; intl rollout from 24 Jun, US open Fri 26 Jun; tracking collapsed to ~US$40–51M on a ~US$175M budget; first reactions split; "judge the gate, not the guess" — held honestly, no pre-judged number); secondary Charli XCX *Music, Fashion, Film* announced (24 Jul); Strokes *Reality Awaits* / Muse *The Wow! Signal* both 26 Jun one-line (No. 28); **Olivia Rodrigo CLOSED/saturated**. THE FAMILY DESK (2pp): the LEAD — **protect the sleep** (the June SG school-holiday window as a body-clock reset): a University of Oregon weekend-recovery-sleep/anxiety finding + AAP/AASM 9–12h & regular sleep-wake timing as the load-bearing foundation; a "try this week" box (anchor wake-time ±1h, morning daylight, screens out of the bedroom) + a "don't be alarmist" box (a livable average, not a compliance regime) + the evidence page (parent's own use #1 predictor No. 28; relationships the buffer No. 25–26; affluent-home criticism risk No. 29); **deliberately NOT screen-time** (saturated); general guidance, not medical advice. THE MACRO DESK ("The Big Picture", standing): the chain — FOMC hawkish HOLD 3.50–3.75% (17 Jun, Warsh; No. 27–29 delta) + inflation near a 3-yr high → US 10Y ~4.46% / dollar bid (DXY ~1-yr high) → war premium draining from Brent (~US$77, 3-mo low) & gold (~US$4,140, −25% from the 28 Jan peak) as Hormuz reopens → USD/JPY ~¥161.5 (explains the Good Life soft yen) → **May PCE due Thu 25 Jun = "awaiting the print"** (the spine in a data release); regime = **higher-for-longer / dollar-bid / risk-capped** (No. 28 delta); standing close = positive equity–bond correlation, diversification doing less for **VWRA & global bonds**; VWRA/AGGU held qualitatively (no fabricated prints); education-not-advice. CURIOSITIES: the LEAD — **a door to the Moon's depths**: the South Pole–Aitken impact excavated deep-mantle material near the **Artemis III** landing sites (astronauts may walk on rock from hundreds of km down; ScienceDaily 22 Jun) — "a line to the deep interior opened by an ancient collision"; secondaries = black-hole winds quenching giant galaxies + an early flickering quasar (~850 Myr post-Big-Bang); "neutrino factory" galaxy one-line (No. 28). LOVE & LIFE: the LEAD — **the receding marital horizon** (IFS *State of Our Unions 2026*: marriage still expected by most yet only ~1-in-3 actively dating, the wedding always a few years out — "a blueprint everyone keeps and no one breaks ground on") with a warm mid-40s turn (the long-built relationship is the load-bearing line); self-report/US-sample caveat; No. 19/20 callbacks. FITNESS ("The Long Game"): the LEAD — **measure the structure, don't track the score**: demonstrable strength (grip, chair-stand) predicts survival largely independent of aerobic activity (LaMonte et al., *JAMA Network Open* 2026 — older-women cohort, magnitudes don't transfer) + a no-wearable quarterly field-test (dead-hang / sit-to-stand / timed stairs); sceptic box (observational; grip as marker as much as cause); strength-volume sweet-spot (No. 18) SATURATED callback only; general guidance, not medical advice. THE DIARY (2pp): the marquee ★ = **BIGBANG 20/26 World Tour, National Stadium Sat 17 Oct 2026** — the gates begin to open (VIP presale 24 Jun; global GA on-sale Fri 26 Jun 10am local for the US/Europe dates); **the SG on-sale date/price STILL pending** (register b.stage; the "11 Sep" figure is the New Jersey date, not SG) — honesty box; SG (Dragon Boat 27–28 Jun, GastroBeats/World Cup screenings, DRUM TAO→12 Jul, Children's Festival, NDP 9 Aug, F1+EXO/Mitski autumn) + Japan soft-yen (Mt Fuji opens 1 Jul, Tanabata 7 Jul, Gion 17 & 24 Jul, Tenjin 24–25 Jul, Fuji Rock 24–26 Jul, Sumida late Jul, Nagoya Sumo) + a "planning notes" box (the crowded October: F1 + BIGBANG collide) + a "Plan the flights" box; region (Naadam 11–13 Jul, Rainforest WMF Sarawak, Boryeong Mud). THE TABLE (rotated, fresh, honesty rule): **Carlitos Casa de Comidas** (Spanish, Joo Chiat, suckling pig — the lead, "too new to call") + **Visitors Cafe** (Korean-style brunch, Tanjong Pagar Plaza — viral) + **NiKU iKU** (halal Japanese grill dine-in, Tampines Hub/Lot One — the value/halal pick) + **Freshio Gelato** (2nd outlet, KADA Maxwell — a real new peg); prior venues (Asin, Obongzip, Between Buns, Torikizoku, etc.) not re-listed. PHOTO EDITION heroes (6, all verified from their Commons file pages, honestly labelled): Strait of Hormuz — File:Strait of Hormuz (MODIS 2020-12-04).jpg, PD-NASA/MODIS (representative, leads The World); Marina Bay at dusk — File:Singapore Marina Bay Dusk 2018-02-27.jpg, CC BY-SA 4.0, Benh Lieu Song (representative, Singapore); Servers in a Rack — File:Servers in a Rack.jpg, CC BY-SA 3.0, Abigor (representative, Technology); Mount Fuji from Lake Kawaguchi — File:Mount Fuji from Lake Kawaguchi (2015-10-26).jpg, CC BY-SA 4.0, Alpsdake (representative, The Good Life/Japan); Full Moon — File:FullMoon2010.jpg, CC BY-SA 3.0, Gregory H. Revera (representative, Curiosities); the Golden Spike ceremony — File:East and West Shaking hands at the laying of last rail Union Pacific Railroad.jpg, PD, Andrew J. Russell 1869 (**actual subject**, leads The Long Read). The cover "opening line"/route hero, the circulates-vs-carries diagram, the Thread-mesh figure, the SPA-basin schematic, the Macro market tables, the "ribbon or load" threads figure and all conceptual/data figures kept as original SVG (no forced photo). THE LONG READ (3pp): **"The Opening of the Line"** — Churchill's "we shape our buildings; thereafter they shape us" drawn through the week's openings, anchored to the Metropolitan Railway / first London Underground (10 Jan 1863, the doubted blueprint that bore ~38,000 passengers on day one), the Golden Spike at Promontory Summit (10 May 1869 — the soft gold spike tapped in then pulled out & replaced with iron: "the ceremony used a spike that could not hold a train") and the Tōkaidō Shinkansen (1 Oct 1964, nine days before the Tokyo Olympics — the "outdated" bet that re-made Japan); counter-case = ribbons that never bore load (Kellogg–Briand No. 13, Versailles No. 23); the field-guide = ride it / find the gold spike / what will it shape; callbacks Hegel (No. 29, the verdict comes later), Milton (No. 23, arrival is a beginning), Lao Tzu (No. 22, the useful emptiness — a railway is mostly tunnel). Editorial theme: *the opening of the line / ribbon vs load*.

issues.quote: I turn the wheel that spins; I delight to see the high come down and the low ascend.
issues.author: Boethius, *The Consolation of Philosophy*, Bk II (c. 524) — the voice of Fortune
issues.note: **Weekday Edition** (full twelve-desk weekday book, 24pp; news-forward; no Travel Desk; The Macro Desk standing). Issue spine: *the high-water mark — a peak is confirmable only from its far side; mark the visible water, flag the unseen depth, call neither shoreline nor bottom permanent*. Threads: gold −25% off the 28 Jan ATH, oil −40% off the war peak, the dollar at a 13-month high, the inflation gauge at an expected peak, the UK landslide curdled. THE WORLD (2pp, four viewpoints Washington/Tehran/Vienna-IAEA/Beijing): the LEAD — **one signature, two road maps** — the Islamabad Memorandum (signed remotely 17 Jun) is read two ways: Washington (Vance/Trump: inspectors back "this week") vs Tehran (Baghaei/Gharibabadi: "no clear schedule," only after a final deal); IAEA's Grossi broke silence Wed 24 Jun at Fukushima ("day after tomorrow or in 10 days"); last verified stockpile 13 Jun 2025 = 441 kg 60%-HEU; Hormuz reopening contested (~35–39 vessels Mon 22 Jun ≈ ⅓ of pre-war flow; IMO evacuating 11,000 seafarers; US 60-day waiver freed ~67M bbl, Chinese refiners the biggest buyers). SECONDARY — **Starmer resigned** (Mon 22 Jun; the 2024 174-seat landslide curdled in ~18 months) → Burnham (won Makerfield by-election 18 Jun ~54.8%, Streeting-backed, ~200 MPs; nominations 9–16 Jul; PM ~17–18 Jul; would be the 7th UK leader in a decade) + Lebanon truce frayed (2 killed Tue 24 Jun) + Colombia (de la Espriella 43.7% v Cepeda 40.9%) + Ukraine refinery strikes one-line. SINGAPORE: the LEAD — **May CPI undershot** (services eased; Apr base 1.8% headline / 1.4% core; MAS/MTI hold the 1.5–2.5% band; July review = hold) — "was May the peak, and is June the ebb?"; secondaries = STI record ~5,185 amid the global rout; retirement 63→64 / re-employment 68→69 from 1 Jul; oil collapse easing the energy-tariff lag; NODX +38.4% one-line; nuclear/SMR parked at 2027 (Asia Nuclear & SMR summit SG 30 Jun–1 Jul). PROPERTY: the LEAD — **frozen near the high mark** — the River Valley Green Parcel C GLS (closed 18 Jun, Sunway-MCL × CSC Land S$750.57M / **S$1,730 psf ppr**, 4 bids, a fresh CCR benchmark) set against developer sales at a 3-mo low (447 May vs 1,548 Apr); construction floor >S$2,400 psf; Vela Bay (SingHaiyi, first Bayshore-precinct private launch at Bayshore MRT) ties TEL Stage 4 + the 15 Jul Bayshore GLS; ~22 launches / ~9,700 units 2026 (~85% RCR/OCR); SORA + EC overhaul (No. 28) one-line. TECHNOLOGY (2pp): the LEAD — **a $1.3-trillion stress-test** (Tue 23 Jun, started in Asia: KOSPI −10%, Nikkei −3.5%; Micron −10/11%, Sandisk −11.7%, Marvell −8%, Qualcomm −8.6%, Arm −8.4%, TSMC −5.2%, SMH −6.5%; Nasdaq-100 >7% off its intramonth peak after a 9-wk run; SpaceX −35%/wk, >$600B erased below the $150 IPO open; Cerebras −12%) — Schwab "stress-test not breakdown," the circular-financing read (Nvidia↔OpenAI loop, Alphabet seeking $85bn, a DeepMind scientist → Anthropic); Micron Q3 held honestly (after close 24 Jun; consensus EPS ~$20.38, rev ~$35.5B +280% y/y, GM ~81%, HBM4 for Rubin); H200/China one-line. p8 (The Home Lab): Gemma 4 12B/31B + Ollama 0.30/MLX + LM Studio KV-cache (No. 29) into Home Assistant; the 24GB sweet spot (a 24GB-fit figure: 12B/31B Q4/Q6 vs the ceiling); "16GB is the new floor"; open-weight frontier "still on the shelf." THE KIT: the LEAD — **the wired renaissance** (NAD C 589 CD player + an affordable audiophile turntable + sub-$100 lossless wired IEMs, e.g. the ~$50 Zero:Red) with a sceptic note (wired ≠ strictly better); "still on the shelf" strip (Sony ColleXion $650, Soundcore Liberty 5 Pro Max, ATH-WP900SE × Fujigen, Snap Specs, Meze Arta, WiiM Bar, ROG Xreal R1, Surface Pro X2). CONNECTED HOME: the LEAD — **spec runs ahead of shelf** — Matter 1.6 (17 Jun; setup / multi-ecosystem / thermostat / NFC commissioning, NO new device categories) vs 1.5.1 (31 Mar); the rule = buy the certified device, not the version number; HA matter.js rebuild + Thread mesh view + Matter 1.5.1 (No. 30) and HA 2026.6/2026.5 continuity. THE GOOD LIFE: the LEAD — **the soft-yen Japan window** (USD/JPY ~¥162, SGD/JPY ~¥126; pre-1 Nov tax-free reform; the BoJ-hike debate; a visitor-gift/resident-strain caveat); culture = F.P. Journe Chronomètre à Résonance US$13.9M at Phillips (Robb Report 16 Jun) + an MB&F robot-watch wink; the OBJECT = the **Christopher Ward C60 Pool Diver × Seconde/Seconde/** (12 Jun, value-not-flex). SCREEN & SOUND: the LEAD — **Supergirl** (Milly Alcock; dir Gillespie; prod Gunn/Safran) — tracking slid from $55M+ to $39–51M on a $175M budget (~$315M break-even), reviews "mixed-positive" lifting ~25 Jun, ~$100M promo / 80+ brands; intl from 24 Jun, US 26 Jun; "gate not guess," no pre-judged gross; secondaries = Strokes *Reality Awaits* + Muse *The Wow! issues.quote: Every day is a journey, and the journey itself is home.

issues.note: **The Weekend Edition** (Sunday lifestyle-mode book, 28pp = the fixed twelve-desk 24pp book + the 4-page **Travel Desk** supplement; cover + 3 teasers led from Screen & Sound / Curiosities / The Grand Tour; hard-news desks lean & reflective). issues.quote: “Though the mills of God grind slowly, yet they grind exceeding small; / Though with patience He stands waiting, with exactness grinds He all.”
issues.author: Henry Wadsworth Longfellow, “Retribution” (1845), rendering Friedrich von Logau’s *Sinngedichte* (1654)
issues.note: **The Weekday Briefing** (standard 24pp news edition — no Travel Desk, Macro Desk standing). Issue spine: *the lag / the delayed reckoning — every cause keeps its consequence waiting; the bill, the harvest and the reading all arrive late*. Theme tag: *the bill comes due late*. THE WORLD (2pp, 4 viewpoint panels Washington/Tehran/Vienna-IAEA/Doha-Gulf): LEAD “one signature, two clocks” — the Iran 60-day deal roadmap (Doha talks resumed 1 Jul, Vance “going well,” Bürgenstock format) reading fast on paper but slow on the water: Hormuz ~35 vessels/day (⅓ of the ~110 pre-war), ~80 uncleared mines, GPS spoofing easing, the IRGC still warning, a container ship aground; ~441kg 60%-HEU last verified ~Jun 2025; Brent low-$70s, the war premium gone. p4 SECONDARY = FIFA World Cup R16 (today 6 Jul: Portugal–Spain Arlington, USMNT–Belgium Seattle; final 19 Jul MetLife) as America’s 250th “other party” (Infantino) + in-brief (Lebanon truce fraying, Ukraine refinery strikes, Colombia runoff, DXY ~101 softening, ~11,000 stranded seafarers). SINGAPORE: LEAD “the war reaches your meter” — the 1 Jul electricity tariff **+17.0%** (regulated 31.91¢/kWh before GST, 34.78¢ with; 4-room HDB +$17.14/mo) as the delayed bill — set from the 1 Apr–15 Jun gas-price window (the war weeks), 95% of power from imported gas, relief not until Q4 / possibly Q1 2027; U-Save up to $190 in July (>1M households) BUT multi-property owners INELIGIBLE (the reader eats the full increase); cross-ref World/Macro/Connected Home. Secondaries: STI record 5,244.29 (3 Jul, +30.8% YoY) vs soft SGD ~1.292; MTI 2.0–4.0% growth (Q1 6.0%); 1 Jul CPF/retirement changes one-line.
